description: Phase 28: Enhanced Yjs Realtime Collaboration Server with Postgres persistence, S3 snapshots, awareness protocol, per-paragraph permissions, version history, branching, whiteboard support, and code editing. Enhance apps/quantdocs/backend/services/yjs-server.ts and add new collaboration modules.
steps: 1. Enhance apps/quantdocs/backend/services/yjs-server.ts with: (a) Postgres persistence - on flush (configurable interval or on last client disconnect), serialize Y.Doc state and write to a yjs_documents table via a PersistenceAdapter interface. (b) S3 snapshot support - daily snapshot method that encodes full doc state and uploads to S3 via a StorageAdapter interface. (c) Awareness protocol - track per-user cursor positions, selections, and user metadata (name, color) using a Map<docId, Map<clientId, AwarenessState>>. Broadcast awareness updates to all connected clients. (d) Per-paragraph permissions - store permission rules per Y.XmlFragment/paragraph ID (owner, editors, viewers) and validate updates against permissions before applying. (e) Version history with named checkpoints - store snapshots with user-supplied names, allow listing history and restoring to any checkpoint. (f) Doc branching (experimental) - fork a Y.Doc into a new branch by cloning state, track branches per document, allow merging branch back by computing diff.

2. Create apps/quantdocs/backend/services/collab-persistence.ts: PersistenceAdapter class with methods: saveDoc(docId, state: Uint8Array), loadDoc(docId): Uint8Array | null, listVersions(docId), createCheckpoint(docId, name, state). Uses an injectable database client interface.

3. Create apps/quantdocs/backend/services/collab-storage.ts: StorageAdapter class with methods: uploadSnapshot(docId, state: Uint8Array, metadata), downloadSnapshot(docId, version?), listSnapshots(docId). Uses injectable S3-like client interface.

4. Create apps/quantdocs/backend/services/awareness.service.ts: Awareness protocol service that manages per-document awareness state. Methods: updateAwareness(docId, clientId, state), removeClient(docId, clientId), getAwareness(docId): AwarenessState[]. AwarenessState includes userId, cursor position, selection range, user name, user color.

5. Create apps/quantdocs/backend/services/paragraph-permissions.service.ts: Per-paragraph permission enforcement. Methods: setPermission(docId, paragraphId, userId, role), checkPermission(docId, paragraphId, userId, action): boolean, getPermissions(docId, paragraphId). Roles: owner, editor, viewer. Actions: read, write, delete.

6. Create apps/quantdocs/backend/services/version-history.service.ts: Version history with named checkpoints. Methods: createCheckpoint(docId, name, userId), listCheckpoints(docId), restoreCheckpoint(docId, checkpointId): Uint8Array, diffCheckpoints(checkpointA, checkpointB).

7. Create apps/quantdocs/backend/services/doc-branching.service.ts: Experimental branching for docs. Methods: createBranch(docId, branchName, userId), listBranches(docId), getBranchState(branchId), mergeBranch(branchId, targetDocId). Fork by cloning Y.Doc state into a new entry.

8. Create apps/quantdocs/backend/services/whiteboard.service.ts: Yjs-backed infinite canvas (tldraw-style). Methods: createCanvas(docId), addShape(docId, shape), updateShape(docId, shapeId, delta), deleteShape(docId, shapeId), getShapes(docId). Uses Y.Map for shapes, Y.Array for layer ordering. Shape types: rect, ellipse, line, arrow, text, freehand, image.

9. Create apps/quantdocs/backend/services/code-collab.service.ts: Realtime code editing support (CodeMirror 6 + Yjs adapter). Methods: createCodeDoc(docId, language), getCodeContent(docId), applyCodeUpdate(docId, update). Uses Y.Text with language metadata. Supports syntax-aware operations.

10. Create apps/quantdocs/backend/services/collab-mentions.service.ts: Mentions that trigger cross-app notifications. Methods: detectMentions(text): MentionedUser[], notifyMention(docId, mentionedUserId, mentionerUserId, context). Emits notification events that integrate with the Phase 27 notification center.
11. Create apps/quantdocs/backend/__tests__/yjs-collab-persistence.test.ts: Test persistence adapter - save/load round-trip, checkpoint creation and listing, restore from checkpoint.
12. Create apps/quantdocs/backend/__tests__/awareness.service.test.ts: Test awareness - update/remove clients, get all awareness states for a doc, stale cleanup.
13. Create apps/quantdocs/backend/__tests__/paragraph-permissions.test.ts: Test per-paragraph permissions - set/check/deny unauthorized writes.
14. Create apps/quantdocs/backend/__tests__/version-history.test.ts: Test version history - create checkpoint, list, restore content matches original.
15. Create apps/quantdocs/backend/__tests__/doc-branching.test.ts: Test branching - create branch from doc, modify branch independently, merge back.
16. Create apps/quantdocs/backend/__tests__/whiteboard.test.ts: Test whiteboard - add shapes, update, delete, Yjs-backed concurrent edits converge.
17. Create apps/quantdocs/backend/__tests__/code-collab.test.ts: Test code collaboration - create code doc, apply updates, concurrent edits merge correctly.
18. Verify: Run pnpm turbo typecheck --filter=@quant/quantdocs and pnpm turbo test --filter=@quant/quantdocs to confirm all gates pass.
acceptance_criteria: Yjs server persists doc state via PersistenceAdapter (save/load round-trip tested)
S3 snapshot upload/download works via StorageAdapter (unit tested with mock)
Awareness protocol tracks cursors and selections per client per doc
Version history supports named checkpoints with restore
Doc branching creates independent forks that can merge back
Whiteboard shapes are Yjs-backed and converge under concurrent edits
Code collaboration uses Y.Text and merges concurrent edits
10 concurrent editors scenario tested - no merge conflicts, no lost ops (via Yjs CRDT guarantee)
All existing yjs-server.test.ts tests continue to pass
findings: All 147 tests pass (17 test files). Typecheck passes. Existing yjs-server.test.ts tests all pass unchanged. Pre-commit hook enforces header-max-length of 100 chars. Prettier runs via lint-staged. YjsServer constructor now accepts optional adapters via YjsServerOptions interface - fully backward compatible (no args = same behavior as before).
